docs.title: Sparrow Lab
docs.text:
Welcome to my technical hub. I'm Valery Vorobyev, a Python Developer in the making.

This site serves as my personal knowledge base and a showcase of my journey from zero to a professional Backend Engineer.

docs.title: 🚀 My Focus
docs.text: I'm currently mastering Python backend development with a strong emphasis on: - Clean Code: Following PEP 8 and industry best practices. - Automation: Writing scripts to solve real-world problems. - Technical English: Building my career in a global environment.

docs.title: 🛠️ Projects & Guides
docs.text:
• YouTube Audio Guide — My first guide on using "yt-dlp" for efficient learning.
  • Python Roadmap — (Coming soon) My progress through core concepts and OOP.
  • Automation Scripts — (Coming soon) Tools I build to optimize my workflow.

docs.title: 📜 My Principles
docs.text:
1. Manual Coding: No copy-paste, no autocompletion. Every line is typed by hand.
  2. Consistency: Learning and coding every single day.
  3. Transparency: Documenting everything I learn here.

Feel free to connect with me on LinkedIn or check my code on GitHub.

docs.title: How to download YouTube audio for learning
docs.text: In 2026, many standard tools for downloading from YouTube are failing due to frequent API changes. To keep my learning process smooth and get audio for my offline practice, I use yt-dlp. This is a professional-grade command-line tool.

docs.title: 1. Installation
docs.text:
The most reliable way to get the latest version (to avoid HTTP 403 errors) is to download the binary directly from the official GitHub repository:

cd ~/Downloads
wget https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/releases/latest/download/yt-dlp_linux
chmod +x yt-dlp_linux
sudo mv yt-dlp_linux /usr/local/bin/yt-dlp

docs.title: 2. Prerequisites
docs.text:
For audio conversion and high-quality processing, you must have FFmpeg installed on your Linux Mint system:

sudo apt update
sudo apt install ffmpeg -y

docs.title: 3. Recommended Command
docs.text:
To download the best available audio and convert it to a high-quality MP3 (320kbps), use the following syntax:

yt-dlp -f bestaudio --extract-audio --audio-format mp3 --audio-quality 0 "URL"

docs.title: 4. Time-Saving Automation (Bash Alias)
docs.text:
Instead of typing a long command every time, I created an alias. Add this line to your ~/.bashrc file:

alias yta='yt-dlp -f bestaudio --extract-audio --audio-format mp3 --audio-quality 0'

After restarting your terminal, you can download any lesson by simply typing:

yta "https://www.youtube.com/..."

This guide is part of my technical journey in Sparrow Lab.
